conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 5 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Leeds.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you.

**Do you live with Alex, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Just to complete my assessment, I have one more question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 5 years old
- You live in the UK (Leeds)
- Alex lives at home with you
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're usually responsible for a child if you live with them, which is the case here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the key requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old (he's 5)
- You live in the UK
- You're responsible for Alex as he lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can proceed with your Child Benefit application. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 5 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old (under 16), lives in the UK with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 5-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and he is under 16. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 5 years old (under 16), lives in the UK with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 5-year-old child Alex. They are responsible for Alex, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and he is under 16. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
